title: Name the quantity before writing the symbol
goal: Translate average, price, count, and pattern situations into variable expressions while keeping each symbol tied to a named quantity.
firstTutorQuestion: Before writing an expression, what quantity should the variable represent in this situation?
operatorFocus: Keep the learner naming each quantity first instead of jumping straight to shorthand.
watchFor: Learner uses the same variable for different quantities.
Learner writes only a number when the task still asks for an expression.
exampleTutorMove: Ask the learner to say what x means in one sentence before writing the relationship with x.
successSignal: Learner defines the variable clearly and writes a matching expression from the context.

title: Read each term by role
goal: Identify coefficient, constant term, term count, and like terms by reading each part of the expression by role.
firstTutorQuestion: In this expression, which part is the coefficient and which part is the constant term?
operatorFocus: Make the learner separate term roles before any simplification starts.
watchFor: Learner confuses a coefficient with the number of terms.
Learner groups unlike terms because they look similar on the surface.
exampleTutorMove: Cover most of the expression and ask the learner to point to one term at a time and name its role.
successSignal: Learner can identify term roles and distinguish like terms without relying on a guess.

title: Substitute, then compute
goal: Preserve sign, exponent, reciprocal, and grouping structure after substitution before computing the final value.
firstTutorQuestion: After replacing the variable, which signs or parentheses do we still need to protect?
operatorFocus: Separate substitution from simplification and make the learner keep the structure visible.
watchFor: Learner replaces the value correctly but drops a negative sign or exponent.
Learner rewrites the substituted expression into a different structure before computing.
exampleTutorMove: Have the learner rewrite the full substituted expression once before doing any arithmetic.
successSignal: Learner substitutes accurately and evaluates without losing sign, grouping, or reciprocal meaning.

title: Simplify without breaking structure
goal: Distribute correctly, remove parentheses safely, and combine only like terms in a linear expression.
firstTutorQuestion: Which terms stay alike after we remove the parentheses, and which ones must stay separate?
operatorFocus: Slow down structure changes before collecting terms.
watchFor: Learner removes parentheses but does not track the outside sign or factor.
Learner combines terms with different variable parts or different degrees.
exampleTutorMove: Ask the learner to mark like terms after rewriting the expression with parentheses removed.
successSignal: Learner rewrites the expression safely and combines only the terms that match structurally.

title: Build the expression from the story
goal: Model average, concentration, geometry, and counting situations with an expression and then simplify it consistently.
firstTutorQuestion: What relationship between the quantities should the expression show before we simplify anything?
operatorFocus: Keep the learner translating the story into named quantities before arithmetic starts.
watchFor: Learner chooses the right quantities but the wrong operation relationship.
Learner writes a reasonable first expression but cannot simplify it cleanly.
exampleTutorMove: Ask the learner to label the quantities in the story first, then write the expression from those labels.
successSignal: Learner writes a context-matching expression and simplifies it into a usable final form.
